betterquesting:10.desc:8:
When crops breed, the resulting stats are the average of the parents' stats plus a random value between -2 and 4.

Essentially, the variation in stats is a fixed (-2 -> +4). If all parents have fertilizer in them, stat loss is prevented in all cases (0 -> 4). 

Deterministic breeding is similar to how bees work, where you must bring the right species together. Some situations require more than two parents.

[note]Advanced crops (§5MV§r and above materials) will require the breeding machine.[/note]

Most crops will also have access to breeding pools, where similar crops may be bred with others of the same “type” to produce other similar crops. When first starting out, it can be helpful to use these pools to collect many different types of crops. They are a bit more complicated so we will get into them after you breed the stonelily.

If you find the process of breeding new crops deterministically too slow, maybe try statting up one species and using it to create a bunch of 31/31/31 seeds for crops in pools it shares. Remember that fertilizer prevents stat loss in all cases, this includes breeding.
